title: England defeats France 6-4 and takes third place in the World Cup
summary: In a 10-goal match, England secured its first third-place finish in the World Cup with an offensive display; Mbappé scored two goals to reach 10 in the tournament.

title: Expectations for the World Cup final: Argentina vs Spain
summary: The Albiceleste team seeks the back-to-back championship in Messi's land, while Spain trusts its young promise Lamine Yamal to return to the top after 16 years.

title: United States bombs Iranian positions for eighth consecutive day after attack in Jordan that killed two soldiers
summary: In retaliation for the drone attack on the Jordanian border, the US Central Command intensified its airstrikes against targets in Iran.

title: Bear sightings increase in several prefectures of Japan
summary: Incidents with bears are reported in Ibaraki, Kobe, and Hokkaido, including the hunting of a specimen by a wounded hunter and the appearance of a bear near a train station.

title: Japanese baseball players shine both in MLB and NPB
summary: Seiya Suzuki extends his hitting streak with the Cubs, Tomoyuki Sugano achieves his ninth win in NPB, and the Giants struggle to score despite 13 hits.

title: Several criminal incidents shake Japan: nurse arrested, abandoned bodies, and armed woman
summary: A former nurse is arrested for allegedly mixing feces in intravenous saline, two women's bodies are found in Nara, and a woman with a knife is subdued by police near a station.

title: Japan boosts its defense with export of destroyers, unmanned ships, and nuclear debate
summary: The government advances the export of used destroyers, develops unmanned ships for the coast guard, and the Defense Minister mentions the need to discuss nuclear capabilities.

title: Technological advances: quantum neural networks, artificial intelligence in football, and digital analysis in baseball
summary: Japanese scientists propose a new type of quantum neural network, AI is used for tactics in the World Cup, and women's baseball uses digital analysis to improve performance.

title: Economic trends: youth growth in Southeast Asia, investment plan, and iPhone price hike
summary: Southeast Asia gains demographic weight, the government promotes a public-private investment plan, and Apple increases the iPhone price in Japan due to the weak yen.

title: Health and wellness: egg freezing, cancer film, and child mental health
summary: Olympic medalist Natsumi Kagami opts for egg cryopreservation, a film based on the diary of a woman who died of cancer is released, and a warning is issued about the lack of child mental health specialists.

title: Community initiatives in Japan: café for children, candy store, and affordable breakfasts
summary: A special cleaning company opens a children's café at a crime scene, students create a candy store on the shopping street, and a university offers balanced breakfasts for 100 yen.

title: Sports news: badminton, sumo, golf, and ekiden
summary: Badminton player Watanabe advances to the Japan Open final, sumo wrestler Takayasu achieves a victory, Hideki Matsuyama moves up positions at the British Open, and an ekiden competition is held.

title: Kylian Mbappé sets new record as first player to score double digits in a World Cup since 1966
summary: The French forward now has 22 World Cup goals, surpasses Lionel Messi by two goals, and becomes the tournament's top scorer with 10 goals.

title: Ukraine attacks a warehouse of Russian company Wildberries, causing losses of 104 billion yen
summary: The Ukrainian army struck a logistics center of Russia's main online store, causing a large-scale fire.

title: Japan issues warnings for extreme heat in the west and heavy rain in the north
summary: Authorities warn of dangerous temperatures in western and northwestern regions, while Hokkaido and Aomori face risk of floods and landslides.

title: Landslide destroys a home in Ashikaga city, Tochigi
summary: Intense rains caused a landslide that buried a house; rescue teams search for a missing woman.

title: Japanese rugby team suffers a heavy defeat against France
summary: The Japanese team showed a lower-than-expected performance and fell by a wide margin to the French, highlighting the gap in level.

title: Debate reopens on imperial succession in Japan
summary: Former Prime Minister Yoshihiko Noda calls it a 'personal defeat' not to have opened the way for an empress, while the government reviews the Imperial Household Law.

title: Construction of the Linear Shinkansen begins in Shizuoka after years of delay
summary: The governor of Shizuoka said 'this is the true beginning' and a committee was formed to oversee the work on the controversial magnetic levitation train line.

title: Government makes special concessions to Saga prefecture for the Kyushu Shinkansen
summary: Given the particular circumstances, the state yielded to Saga's demands to advance the bullet train project on Kyushu island.
